steps.title: Use Alexander's schedule
steps.content: Alexander is very busy, works almost constantly, avoids clubs, and usually dines alone. The context emphasizes that work occupies nearly all his time.

steps.title: Reject wrong meanings
steps.content: Choice A means in a way too slight to notice. Choice B means fearfully. Choice D means hurriedly. The passage is about duration and frequency, not invisibility, fear, or speed.

steps.title: Choose without stopping
steps.content: Continuously means in an ongoing, nearly uninterrupted way, which fits Alexander being at work almost all the time. The answer is C.
